---
title: "About This Project"
---

## Shadow Tealbook A

This is a research prototype developed at **Purdue University** that replicates 
the structure and analytical approach of the Federal Reserve's Tealbook A — the 
internal briefing document prepared for each FOMC meeting.

### What it does

- Pulls current economic data automatically from FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)
- Generates Tealbook-style interpretive narratives using a large language model (Claude, Anthropic)
- Identifies potential special topics using live Federal Reserve speech and working paper feeds
- Rerenders automatically on a schedule via GitHub Actions

### Data sources

| Source | Series |
|--------|---------|
| FRED (St. Louis Fed) | GDP, unemployment, inflation, financial conditions |
| Dallas Fed | Trimmed mean PCE |
| BLS via FRED | Import prices, ECI, AHE, JOLTS |
| BEA via FRED | PCE components |
| Federal Reserve | Speeches (RSS), FEDS working papers (RSS) |
| Philadelphia Fed | Survey of Professional Forecasters (SPF) — manually updated |
| FOMC | Longer-run SEP — manually updated each cycle |

### Methodology notes

- All FRED pulls use a local parquet cache (refreshed every 12 hours)
- LLM narratives are regenerated at each render using current data
- Forecasts (SPF, FOMC SEP) are hard-coded and updated manually each FOMC cycle
- International GDP section pending IMF IFS API integration
- Financial markets section uses FRED; equity data integration pending

### Disclaimer

This document is a **research project** and does not represent the views of the 
Federal Reserve System, the Federal Open Market Committee, or any Federal Reserve 
Bank. It is produced solely for academic purposes.

---
*Purdue University — MGMT/Economics Research*
